title: Camera unit for an electronic pen
abstract: Acamera unit (4) for anelectronic pencomprises a housing (9) with a front face (11), an inlet opening (13) in the front face (11), and a compartment (10) which extends from the inlet opening (13) and includes a mounting portion (14)for an image sensor (15). The camera unit (4) further comprises an irradiating device (19), an imaging system that defines an optical axis (OA) extending through the inlet opening (13) to the image sensor (15), and a sheet device (20) which is attached to the front face (11) of the housing (9) across the inlet opening (13) to define a radiation-transmitting aperture (21) on the optical axis(OA). The sheet device (20) is arranged to form a shield to unwanted radiation and/or define the aperture stop of the imaging system. The irradiatingdevice (19) is directly or indirectly attached to the front face (11) to enable a compact camera unit (4).
claims:
Claims 1. A camera unit for an electronic pen, the camera unit comprising: a housing (9) that defines a front face (11), an inlet opening (13) in the front face (11), and a compartment (10) which extends from the inlet opening (13) and includes a sensor mounting portion (14); an image sensor (15) arranged at the sensor mounting portion (14); an imaging system that defines an optical axis (OA) that extends through the inlet opening (13) to the image sensor (15), wherein the imaging system comprises an imaging lens (16) in the compartment (10); a sheet device (20) that defines a radiation-transmitting aperture (21), said sheet device (20) being attached to the front face (11) of the housing (9) across the inlet opening (13) and with the radiation-transmitting aperture (21) on the optical axis (OA) of the imaging system; and an irradiating device (19) which is directly or indirectly attached to the front face (11). 2. The camera unit of claim 1, wherein the irradiating device (19) is attached to the sheet device (20) to at least partly overlap the front face (11) of the housing (9). 3. The camera unit of claim 1 or 2, wherein the irradiating device (19) is arranged with a spacing from the radiation-transmitting aperture (21). 4. The camera unit of any preceding claim, wherein the sheet device (20) comprises electrical conductors (27), and wherein the irradiating device (19) is electrically connected to the electrical conductors (27). 5. The camera unit of claim 4, wherein the irradiating device (19) is electrically connected to the electrical conductors (27) by being placed on the sheet device (20) in electrical contact with the electrical conductors (27). 6. The camera unit of claim 4 or 5, wherein the electrical conductors (27) are arranged as an external or internal layer of the sheet device (20). 7. The camera unit of any preceding claim, wherein the sheet device (20) comprises a layer (32) of conductive material for heat dissipation, and wherein a heat transfer portion of the irradiating device (19) is arranged in contact with the layer (32) of conductive material. 8. The camera unit of claim 7, wherein the layer (32) of conductive material extends from the perimeter of the radiation-transmitting aperture (21) to the irradiating device (19) and is non-transparent to radiation emitted by the irradiating device (19). 9. The camera unit of any preceding claim, wherein the sheet device (20) is a flexible circuit. 10. The camera unit of any preceding claim, wherein the irradiating device (19) is a surface-mount device, SMD. 11. The camera unit of any preceding claim, wherein the sheet device (20) has a maximum thickness at the perimeter of the radiation-transmitting aperture (21) of 0.5 mm or less, more preferably 0.4 mm or less, and most preferably 0.3 mm or less. 12. The camera unit of any preceding claim, wherein the sheet device (20) is arranged to block radiation in the wavelength range of the irradiating device (19), and wherein the radiation-transmitting aperture (21) is configured to transmit radiation in the wavelength range of the irradiating device (19). 13. The camera unit of any preceding claim, wherein the radiation-transmitting aperture (21) is formed as a through-hole in the sheet device (20). 14. The camera unit of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the radiation-transmitting aperture (17) comprises a window of radiation-transparent material surrounded by non- transparent material. 15. The camera unit of any preceding claim, wherein the front face (11) comprises a support surface (9") which defines the inlet opening (13), wherein the sheet device (20) is directly or indirectly attached to the support surface (9"), and wherein the radiation-transmitting aperture (21) is smaller than and overlaps the inlet opening (13). 16. The camera unit of claim 15, wherein the support surface (9") is inclined towards the optical axis (OA), at least in a region beneath the irradiating device (19). 17. The camera unit of claim 15 or 16, wherein the support surface (9") comprises guiding pins (31A-31C), and wherein the sheet device (20) comprises guiding holes (30A-30C) for engagement with the guiding pins (31 A-31C). 18. The camera unit of any preceding claim, wherein the housing (9) is further configured to define an opening (17; 23) on the optical axis (OA) inside the compartment (10), wherein the imaging system further comprises an aperture stop and wherein the opening (17) is the aperture stop of the imaging system. 19. The camera unit of any one of claims 1-18, wherein the imaging system comprises an aperture stop and wherein the radiation-transmitting aperture (21) is the aperture stop of the imaging system. 20. An electronic pen, comprising: a stylus (3); the camera unit (4) of any preceding claim, which is arranged to generate an image of a surface (S) in temporary engagement with the stylus (3); a power source (7), which is electrically connected to the image sensor (15) and the irradiating device (19) of the camera unit (4); and 

 a control unit (6), which is configured to operate the irradiating device (19) to emit radiation onto the surface (S) and to operate the image sensor (15) to acquire the image.
